links.title: MOOSE-Star: Unlocking Tractable Training for Scientific Discovery by Breaking the Complexity Barrier

primary_link.title: MOOSE-Star: Unlocking Tractable Training for Scientific Discovery by Breaking the Complexity Barrier

papers.summary: While large language models (LLMs) show promise in scientific discovery, existing research focuses on inference or feedback-driven training, leaving the direct modeling of the generative reasoning process, P(hypothesis|background) (P(h|b)), unexplored. We demonstrate that directly training P(h|b) is mathematically intractable due to the combinatorial complexity (O(N^k)) inherent in retrieving and composing inspirations from a vast knowledge base. To break this barrier, we introduce MOOSE-Star, a unified framework enabling tractable training and scalable inference. In the best case, MOOSE-Star reduces complexity from exponential to logarithmic (O(log N)) by (1) training on decomposed subtasks derived from the probabilistic equation of discovery, (2) employing motivation-guided hierarchical search to enable logarithmic retrieval and prune irrelevant subspaces, and (3) utilizing bounded composition for robustness against retrieval noise. To facilitate this, we release TOMATO-Star, a dataset of 108,717 decomposed papers (38,400 GPU hours) for training. Furthermore, we show that while brute-force sampling hits a ''complexity wall,'' MOOSE-Star exhibits continuous test-time scaling.
papers.summary_zh: 儘管 large language models (LLMs) 在科學發現中展現了前景，但現有研究主要集中於 inference 或 feedback-driven training，而生成式推理過程 P(hypothesis|background) (P(h|b)) 的直接建模仍未被探索。我們證明了直接訓練 P(h|b) 在數學上是難以處理的，因為從龐大的知識庫中檢索和組合靈感時存在固有的組合複雜性 (O(N^k))。為了突破這一障礙，我們引入了 MOOSE-Star，這是一個統一的框架，可實現可行訓練和可擴展 inference。在最佳情況下，MOOSE-Star 將複雜性從指數級降低到對數級 (O(log N))，方法是：(1) 在源自發現概率方程的 decomposed subtasks 上進行訓練；(2) 採用 motivation-guided hierarchical search 以實現對數級檢索並修剪不相關的子空間；(3) 利用 bounded composition 來提高對 retrieval noise 的穩健性。為了促進這一點，我們發布了 TOMATO-Star，一個包含 108,717 篇 decomposed papers 的數據集 (38,400 GPU hours) 用於訓練。此外，我們還表明，雖然 brute-force sampling 會遇到「複雜性牆」，但 MOOSE-Star 展現了 continuous test-time scaling。
papers.title: MOOSE-Star: Unlocking Tractable Training for Scientific Discovery by Breaking the Complexity Barrier
papers.title_zh: MOOSE-Star：透過突破複雜性障礙，實現科學發現的可行訓練

links.title: SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via Continuous Integration

primary_link.title: SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via Continuous Integration

papers.summary: Large language model (LLM)-powered agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in automating software engineering tasks such as static bug fixing, as evidenced by benchmarks like SWE-bench. However, in the real world, the development of mature software is typically predicated on complex requirement changes and long-term feature iterations -- a process that static, one-shot repair paradigms fail to capture. To bridge this gap, we propose SWE-CI, the first repository-level benchmark built upon the Continuous Integration loop, aiming to shift the evaluation paradigm for code generation from static, short-term functional correctness toward dynamic, long-term maintainability. The benchmark comprises 100 tasks, each corresponding on average to an evolution history spanning 233 days and 71 consecutive commits in a real-world code repository. SWE-CI requires agents to systematically resolve these tasks through dozens of rounds of analysis and coding iterations. SWE-CI provides valuable insights into how well agents can sustain code quality throughout long-term evolution.
papers.summary_zh: 由大型語言模型 (LLM) 驅動的代理已在自動化軟體工程任務（例如靜態錯誤修復）方面展現出強大的能力，如 SWE-bench 等基準測試所示。然而，在現實世界中，成熟軟體的開發通常基於複雜的需求變更和長期的功能迭代——這是一個靜態、一次性修復範式無法捕捉的過程。為彌補這一差距，我們提出了 SWE-CI，這是第一個基於 Continuous Integration 循環的儲存庫級別基準，旨在將程式碼生成的評估範式從靜態、短期功能正確性轉變為動態、長期可維護性。該基準包含 100 個任務，每個任務平均對應於真實世界程式碼儲存庫中跨越 233 天和 71 次連續提交的演進歷史。SWE-CI 要求代理透過數十輪分析和程式碼迭代系統地解決這些任務。SWE-CI 為代理在長期演進中如何良好地維持程式碼品質提供了寶貴的見解。

links.title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery LLM Agents

primary_link.title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery LLM Agents

papers.summary:
Tool-augmented large language model (LLM) agents promise to unify scientific reasoning with computation, yet their deployment in high-stakes domains like drug discovery is bottlenecked by two critical barriers: unconstrained tool-use governance and poor long-horizon reliability. In dependency-heavy pharmaceutical pipelines, autonomous agents often drift into irreproducible trajectories, where early-stage hallucinations multiplicatively compound into downstream failures. To overcome this, we present Mozi, a dual-layer architecture that bridges the flexibility of generative AI with the deterministic rigor of computational biology. Layer A (Control Plane) establishes a governed supervisor--worker hierarchy that enforces role-based tool isolation, limits execution to constrained action spaces, and drives reflection-based replanning. Layer B (Workflow Plane) operationalizes canonical drug discovery stages -- from Target Identification to Lead Optimization -- as stateful, composable skill graphs. This layer integrates strict data contracts and strategic human-in-the-loop (HITL) checkpoints to safeguard scientific validity at high-uncertainty decision boundaries.
  Operating on the design principle of ``free-form reasoning for safe tasks, structured execution for long-horizon pipelines,'' Mozi provides built-in robustness mechanisms and trace-level audibility to completely mitigate error accumulation. We evaluate Mozi on PharmaBench, a curated benchmark for biomedical agents, demonstrating superior orchestration accuracy over existing baselines. Furthermore, through end-to-end therapeutic case studies, we demonstrate Mozi's ability to navigate massive chemical spaces, enforce stringent toxicity filters, and generate highly competitive in silico candidates, effectively transforming the LLM from a fragile conversationalist into a reliable, governed co-scientist.
papers.summary_zh:
工具增強型大型語言模型 (LLM) Agents 有望將科學推理與計算結合，但在藥物發現等高風險領域中的部署卻受到兩個關鍵障礙的限制：不受約束的工具使用治理和較差的長期可靠性。在依賴性強的製藥管線中，自主 Agents 經常偏離不可重現的軌跡，其中早期階段的「幻覺 (hallucinations)」會乘數般地累積導致下游失敗。為了克服這一點，我們提出了 Mozi，這是一種雙層架構，它將生成式 AI 的靈活性與計算生物學的確定性嚴謹性相結合。A 層 (Control Plane) 建立了受控的監督者-工作者層級結構，該結構強制執行基於角色的工具隔離，將執行限制在受約束的動作空間內，並推動基於反思的重新規劃。B 層 (Workflow Plane) 將規範的藥物發現階段（從 Target Identification 到 Lead Optimization）操作為有狀態的、可組合的技能圖。該層整合了嚴格的數據契約和戰略性 human-in-the-loop (HITL) 檢查點，以在高不確定性決策邊界處保障科學有效性。
Mozi 遵循「安全任務的自由形式推理，長期管線的結構化執行」設計原則，提供了內建的魯棒性機制和追蹤級可審計性，以完全緩解錯誤累積。我們在 PharmaBench（一個為生物醫學 Agents 策劃的基準測試）上評估了 Mozi，展示了其優於現有基準的卓越協調準確性。此外，通過端到端治療案例研究，我們證明了 Mozi 導航巨大化學空間、執行嚴格毒性篩選以及生成極具競爭力的 in silico 候選藥物的能力，有效地將 LLM 從脆弱的對話者轉變為可靠、受控的協同科學家。
papers.title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery LLM Agents

links.title: Specificity-aware reinforcement learning for fine-grained open-world classification

primary_link.title: Specificity-aware reinforcement learning for fine-grained open-world classification

papers.summary: Classifying fine-grained visual concepts under open-world settings, i.e., without a predefined label set, demands models to be both accurate and specific. Recent reasoning Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) exhibit strong visual understanding capability but tend to produce overly generic predictions when performing fine-grained image classification. Our preliminary analysis reveals that models do possess the intrinsic fine-grained domain knowledge. However, promoting more specific predictions (specificity) without compromising correct ones (correctness) remains a non-trivial and understudied challenge. In this work, we investigate how to steer reasoning LMMs toward predictions that are both correct and specific. We propose a novel specificity-aware reinforcement learning framework, SpeciaRL, to fine-tune reasoning LMMs on fine-grained image classification under the open-world setting. SpeciaRL introduces a dynamic, verifier-based reward signal anchored to the best predictions within online rollouts, promoting specificity while respecting the model's capabilities to prevent incorrect predictions. Our out-of-domain experiments show that SpeciaRL delivers the best trade-off between correctness and specificity across extensive fine-grained benchmarks, surpassing existing methods and advancing open-world fine-grained image classification. Code and model are publicly available at https://github.com/s-angheben/SpeciaRL.

links.title: Bielik-Q2-Sharp: A Comparative Study of Extreme 2-bit Quantization Methods for a Polish 11B Language Model

primary_link.title: Bielik-Q2-Sharp: A Comparative Study of Extreme 2-bit Quantization Methods for a Polish 11B Language Model

radar.summary: We present Bielik-Q2-Sharp, the first systematic academic evaluation of extreme 2-bit quantization applied to a Polish large language model. Using Bielik-11B-v2.3-Instruct (11B parameters, Mistral architecture) as our base model, we compare six state-of-the-art post-training quantization methods -- QuIP#, SpinQuant+GPTQ, ButterflyQuant, QTIP, VPTQ, and AQLM -- all calibrated on a Polish-language corpus (CulturaX-PL) with shared Hessian matrices. Our best variant (QuIP# E8P12) achieves 71.92% acr
radar.summary_zh: 我們提出了 Bielik-Q2-Sharp，這是首次對應用於波蘭語大型語言模型的極端 2-bit quantization 進行系統性學術評估。我們使用 Bielik-11B-v2.3-Instruct (11B 參數，Mistral architecture) 作為我們的基礎模型，比較了六種最先進的 post-training quantization 方法——QuIP#、SpinQuant+GPTQ、ButterflyQuant、QTIP、VPTQ 和 AQLM——所有這些方法都在一個波蘭語語料庫 (CulturaX-PL) 上使用共享 Hessian matrices 進行校準。我們最佳的變體 (QuIP# E8P12) 實現了 71.92% 的準確度
radar.title: Bielik-Q2-Sharp: A Comparative Study of Extreme 2-bit Quantization Methods for a Polish 11B Language Model

While LLMs can translate complex physiological signals into clinical narratives, it is unclear how agentic systems perform relative to zero-shot inference. radar.summary_zh: 持續生理監測對緊急護理至關重要，但部署可信賴的 AI 卻充滿挑戰。雖然 LLM 可以將複雜的生理訊號轉化為臨床敘述，但 agentic systems 相對於 zero-shot inference 的表現如何尚不明確。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了 Vivaldi，一個角色結構化的 multi-agent system，用於解釋多變量生理時間序列。由於監管限制排除了即時部署，我們在一個受控環境中實例化了 Vivaldi。

links.title: GIPO: Gaussian Importance Sampling Policy Optimization

primary_link.title: GIPO: Gaussian Importance Sampling Policy Optimization

radar.summary: Post-training with reinforcement learning (RL) has recently shown strong promise for advancing multimodal agents beyond supervised imitation. However, RL remains limited by poor data efficiency, particularly in settings where interaction data are scarce and quickly become outdated. radar.summary_zh: 使用 reinforcement learning (RL) 進行後訓練 (post-training) 最近展現了巨大潛力，可將 multimodal agents 推向超越 supervised imitation 的境界。然而，RL 仍然受限於其數據效率低下，特別是在交互數據稀缺且迅速過時的環境中。為了解決這一挑戰，本文提出 GIPO (Gaussian Importance sampling Policy Optimization) 作為一個策略優化目標，其基於 truncated importance sampling，並以 log-ratio-based 的方式取代了 hard clipping。

links.title: Believe Your Model: Distribution-Guided Confidence Calibration

primary_link.title: Believe Your Model: Distribution-Guided Confidence Calibration

radar.summary: Large Reasoning Models have demonstrated remarkable performance with the advancement of test-time scaling techniques, which enhances prediction accuracy by generating multiple candidate responses and selecting the most reliable answer. While prior work has analyzed that internal model signals like confidence scores can partly indicate response correctness and exhibit a distributional correlation with accuracy, such distributional information has not been fully utilized to guide answer selection.
radar.summary_zh: 隨著 test-time scaling techniques 的進步，Large Reasoning Models 展現了卓越的性能，透過生成多個候選回應並選擇最可靠的答案來提高預測準確性。儘管先前的研究分析指出，模型內部訊號，例如 confidence scores，可以部分指示回應的正確性並與準確性表現出分佈相關性，但此類分佈資訊尚未被充分利用來指導答案選擇。

links.title: T2S-Bench & Structure-of-Thought: Benchmarking and Prompting Comprehensive Text-to-Structure Reasoning

primary_link.title: T2S-Bench & Structure-of-Thought: Benchmarking and Prompting Comprehensive Text-to-Structure Reasoning

top5.summary: Think about how human handles complex reading tasks: marking key points, inferring their relationships, and structuring information to guide understanding and responses. Likewise, can a large language model benefit from text structure to enhance text-processing performance? To explore it, in this work, we first introduce Structure of Thought (SoT), a prompting technique that explicitly guides models to construct intermediate text structures, consistently boosting performance across eight tasks and three model families. Building upon this insight, we present T2S-Bench, the first benchmark designed to evaluate and improve text-to-structure capabilities of models. T2S-Bench includes 1.8K samples across 6 scientific domains and 32 structural types, rigorously constructed to ensure accuracy, fairness, and quality. Evaluation on 45 mainstream models reveals substantial improvement potential: the average accuracy on the multi-hop reasoning task is only 52.1%, and even the most advanced model achieves 58.1% node accuracy in end-to-end extraction. Furthermore, on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, SoT alone yields an average +5.7% improvement across eight diverse text-processing tasks, and fine-tuning on T2S-Bench further increases this gain to +8.6%. These results highlight the value of explicit text structuring and the complementary contributions of SoT and T2S-Bench. Dataset and eval code have been released at https://t2s-bench.github.io/T2S-Bench-Page/.
top5.summary_zh: 思考人類如何處理複雜的閱讀任務：標記關鍵點、推斷其關係，並將信息結構化以指導理解和回應。同樣，大型語言模型是否可以受益於文本結構以提高文本處理性能？為了解決這個問題，在本研究中，我們首先介紹了 Structure of Thought (SoT)，這是一種提示技術，它明確引導模型構建中間文本結構，持續提高八項任務和三個模型系列的性能。基於這一洞察，我們提出了 T2S-Bench，這是第一個旨在評估和改進模型 Text-to-Structure 能力的基準。T2S-Bench 包含來自 6 個科學領域和 32 種結構類型的 1.8K 個樣本，經過嚴格構建以確保準確性、公平性和質量。對 45 個主流模型的評估顯示出巨大的改進潛力：在 multi-hop reasoning 任務上的平均準確度僅為 52.1%，即使是最先進的模型在 end-to-end extraction 中的節點準確度也僅達到 58.1%。此外，在 Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct 上，僅 SoT 就能在八項不同的文本處理任務中平均提高 5.7%，而對 T2S-Bench 進行 fine-tuning 進一步將這一增益提高到 8.6%。這些結果突顯了明確文本結構的價值，以及 SoT 和 T2S-Bench 的互補貢獻。數據集和評估代碼已在 https://t2s-bench.github.io/T2S-Bench-Page/ 發布。
top5.title: T2S-Bench & Structure-of-Thought: Benchmarking and Prompting Comprehensive Text-to-Structure Reasoning
top5.title_zh: T2S-Bench 與 Structure-of-Thought：基準測試和提示綜合性 Text-to-Structure 推理

links.title: RIVER: A Real-Time Interaction Benchmark for Video LLMs

primary_link.title: RIVER: A Real-Time Interaction Benchmark for Video LLMs

top5.summary: The rapid advancement of multimodal large language models has demonstrated impressive capabilities, yet nearly all operate in an offline paradigm, hindering real-time interactivity. Addressing this gap, we introduce the Real-tIme Video intERaction Bench (RIVER Bench), designed for evaluating online video comprehension. RIVER Bench introduces a novel framework comprising Retrospective Memory, Live-Perception, and Proactive Anticipation tasks, closely mimicking interactive dialogues rather than responding to entire videos at once. We conducted detailed annotations using videos from diverse sources and varying lengths, and precisely defined the real-time interactive format. Evaluations across various model categories reveal that while offline models perform well in single question-answering tasks, they struggle with real-time processing. Addressing the limitations of existing models in online video interaction, especially their deficiencies in long-term memory and future perception, we proposed a general improvement method that enables models to interact with users more flexibly in real time. We believe this work will significantly advance the development of real-time interactive video understanding models and inspire future research in this emerging field. Datasets and code are publicly available at https://github.com/OpenGVLab/RIVER.
top5.summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型的快速發展展現了令人印象深刻的能力，但幾乎所有模型都在 offline 範式下運行，阻礙了 real-time interactivity。為了解決這一差距，我們引入了 Real-tIme Video intERaction Bench (RIVER Bench)，它旨在評估 online video comprehension。RIVER Bench 引入了一個新穎的框架，包括 Retrospective Memory、Live-Perception 和 Proactive Anticipation 任務，這些任務密切模仿 interactive dialogues，而不是一次性回應整個視訊。我們使用來自不同來源和不同長度的視訊進行了詳細註釋，並精確定義了 real-time interactive format。對各種模型類別的評估顯示，雖然 offline models 在單一 question-answering 任務中表現良好，但它們在 real-time processing 方面卻力不從心。為了解決現有模型在 online video interaction 中的局限性，特別是它們在 long-term memory 和 future perception 方面的不足，我們提出了一種通用的改進方法，使模型能夠更靈活地與用戶 real time 互動。我們相信這項工作將顯著推動 real-time interactive video understanding models 的發展，並激發這一新興領域的未來研究。數據集和程式碼已在 https://github.com/OpenGVLab/RIVER 公開提供。
top5.title: RIVER: A Real-Time Interaction Benchmark for Video LLMs
top5.title_zh: RIVER：用於 Video LLMs 的實時互動基準

links.title: BeamPERL: Parameter-Efficient RL with Verifiable Rewards Specializes Compact LLMs for Structured Beam Mechanics Reasoning

primary_link.title: BeamPERL: Parameter-Efficient RL with Verifiable Rewards Specializes Compact LLMs for Structured Beam Mechanics Reasoning

top5.summary: Can reinforcement learning with hard, verifiable rewards teach a compact language model to reason about physics, or does it primarily learn to pattern-match toward correct answers? We study this question by training a 1.5B-parameter reasoning model on beam statics, a classic engineering problem, using parameter-efficient RLVR with binary correctness rewards from symbolic solvers, without teacher-generated reasoning traces. The best BeamPERL checkpoint achieves a 66.7% improvement in Pass@1 over the base model. However, the learned competence is anisotropic: the model generalizes compositionally (more loads) but fails under topological shifts (moved supports) that require the same equilibrium equations. Intermediate checkpoints yield the strongest reasoning, while continued optimization degrades robustness while maintaining reward. These findings reveal a key limitation of outcome-level alignment: reinforcement learning with exact physics rewards induces procedural solution templates rather than internalization of governing equations. The precision of the reward signal - even when analytically exact - does not by itself guarantee transferable physical reasoning. Our results suggest that verifiable rewards may need to be paired with structured reasoning scaffolding to move beyond template matching toward robust scientific reasoning.
top5.summary_zh: 使用硬性、可驗證獎勵的 reinforcement learning 能否教導緊湊型語言模型對物理學進行推理，還是它主要學習模式匹配以得出正確答案？我們通過在 beam statics（一個經典的工程問題）上訓練一個 1.5B-parameter reasoning model 來研究這個問題，該模型使用 parameter-efficient RLVR，並從 symbolic solvers 獲得 binary correctness rewards，而無需 teacher-generated reasoning traces。最好的 BeamPERL checkpoint 在 Pass@1 上比 base model 提高了 66.7%。然而，學習到的 competence 是 anisotropic：模型在 compositionally 上（更多 loads）具有泛化性，但在 topological shifts（moved supports）下會失敗，儘管這需要相同的 equilibrium equations。Intermediate checkpoints 產生了最強的 reasoning，而 continued optimization 則 degrades robustness，同時 maintaining reward。這些發現揭示了 outcome-level alignment 的一個關鍵限制：使用 exact physics rewards 的 reinforcement learning 誘導的是 procedural solution templates，而不是 internalization of governing equations。reward signal 的 precision——即使在 analytically exact 的情況下——本身並不能保證 transferable physical reasoning。我們的結果表明，verifiable rewards 可能需要與 structured reasoning scaffolding 結合，才能 move beyond template matching，邁向 robust scientific reasoning。
top5.title: BeamPERL: Parameter-Efficient RL with Verifiable Rewards Specializes Compact LLMs for Structured Beam Mechanics Reasoning
top5.title_zh: BeamPERL：使用可驗證獎勵的參數高效 RL 專門用於緊湊型 LLMs 的結構化梁力學推理

links.title: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B Technical Report

primary_link.title: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B Technical Report

top5.summary: We present Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B, a compact open-weight multimodal reasoning model, and share the motivations, design choices, experiments, and learnings that informed its development. Our goal is to contribute practical insight to the research community on building smaller, efficient multimodal reasoning models and to share the result of these learnings as an open-weight model that is good at common vision and language tasks and excels at scientific and mathematical reasoning and understanding user interfaces. Our contributions include demonstrating that careful architecture choices and rigorous data curation enable smaller, open-weight multimodal models to achieve competitive performance with significantly less training and inference-time compute and tokens. The most substantial improvements come from systematic filtering, error correction, and synthetic augmentation -- reinforcing that data quality remains the primary lever for model performance. Systematic ablations show that high-resolution, dynamic-resolution encoders yield consistent improvements, as accurate perception is a prerequisite for high-quality reasoning. Finally, a hybrid mix of reasoning and non-reasoning data with explicit mode tokens allows a single model to deliver fast direct answers for simpler tasks and chain-of-thought reasoning for complex problems.
top5.summary_zh: 我們介紹了 Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B，這是一個緊湊型 open-weight multimodal reasoning model，並分享了其開發的動機、設計選擇、實驗和經驗。我們的目標是為研究界提供關於構建更小、更高效的 multimodal reasoning models 的實用見解，並將這些經驗的成果作為一個 open-weight model 分享，該模型擅長常見的 vision 和 language tasks，並在 scientific and mathematical reasoning 和 understanding user interfaces 方面表現出色。我們的貢獻包括證明仔細的 architecture 選擇和嚴格的 data curation 能夠使更小型的 open-weight multimodal models 以顯著減少的 training 和 inference-time compute 和 tokens 達到競爭性能。最實質性的改進來自於系統性的 filtering、error correction 和 synthetic augmentation——這強化了數據質量仍然是模型性能的主要槓桿。系統性的 ablations 表明 high-resolution、dynamic-resolution encoders 帶來了持續的改進，因為準確的 perception 是 high-quality reasoning 的先決條件。最後，推理和非推理數據與顯式 mode tokens 的混合組合允許單一模型為簡單任務提供快速直接答案，並為複雜問題提供 chain-of-thought reasoning。
top5.title: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B Technical Report

links.title: Proact-VL: A Proactive VideoLLM for Real-Time AI Companions

primary_link.title: Proact-VL: A Proactive VideoLLM for Real-Time AI Companions

top5.summary: Proactive and real-time interactive experiences are essential for human-like AI companions, yet face three key challenges: (1) achieving low-latency inference under continuous streaming inputs, (2) autonomously deciding when to respond, and (3) controlling both quality and quantity of generated content to meet real-time constraints. In this work, we instantiate AI companions through two gaming scenarios, commentator and guide, selected for their suitability for automatic evaluation. We introduce the Live Gaming Benchmark, a large-scale dataset with three representative scenarios: solo commentary, co-commentary, and user guidance, and present Proact-VL, a general framework that shapes multimodal language models into proactive, real-time interactive agents capable of human-like environment perception and interaction. Extensive experiments show Proact-VL achieves superior response latency and quality while maintaining strong video understanding capabilities, demonstrating its practicality for real-time interactive applications.
top5.summary_zh: Proactive 和 real-time interactive experiences 對於 human-like AI companions 至關重要，但卻面臨三個關鍵挑戰：(1) 在 continuous streaming inputs 下實現 low-latency inference，(2) autonomously 決定何時回應，以及 (3) controlling both quality and quantity of generated content 以滿足 real-time constraints。在這項工作中，我們通過兩種遊戲場景——commentator 和 guide——來實例化 AI companions，這些場景因其適合 automatic evaluation 而被選中。我們介紹了 Live Gaming Benchmark，這是一個大規模數據集，包含三個代表性場景：solo commentary、co-commentary 和 user guidance，並提出了 Proact-VL，這是一個通用框架，可將 multimodal language models 塑造成能夠進行 human-like environment perception 和 interaction 的 proactive、real-time interactive agents。大量的實驗表明，Proact-VL 在保持強大 video understanding capabilities 的同時，實現了卓越的 response latency 和質量，證明了其在 real-time interactive applications 中的實用性。
top5.title: Proact-VL: A Proactive VideoLLM for Real-Time AI Companions
top5.title_zh: Proact-VL：用於實時 AI 夥伴的主動式 VideoLLM
